title: To the Top
lines: On this morning in 1953, two men stood where no one ever had: the top of the world.
They didn't get there in a day. They got there by refusing to stop.
Whatever you're carrying, keep walking. The summit doesn't come to you.

title: Iron Sharpens Iron
lines: As iron sharpens iron, so one man sharpens another. — Proverbs 27:17
Nobody summits alone. Find your rope team and hold the line.
more: line: Type 1+2+1 for the climber's psalm.

title: I Lift My Eyes
lines: I lift up my eyes to the hills. From where does my help come?
My help comes from the LORD, the Maker of heaven and earth. — Psalm 121:1–2
more: line: Type 14×58 for the long haul.

title: Fifty-Eight Peaks
lines: Colorado holds 58 peaks over fourteen thousand feet. Nobody climbs them all in a weekend.
Money grows the same way: one steady climb at a time, and the view keeps getting better.

title: Well spotted
lines: 144: a dozen dozens, stacked like cordwood.

title: Act Like Men
lines: Be watchful, stand firm in the faith, act like men, be strong. — 1 Corinthians 16:13
Four commands. No footnotes.

title: No Spirit of Fear
lines: For God gave us a spirit not of fear but of power and love and self-control. — 2 Timothy 1:7
Fear doesn't get a vote on the mountain.

title: A King's Last Words
lines: I am about to go the way of all the earth. Be strong, and show yourself a man. — 1 Kings 2:2
David to Solomon. Still standing orders.

title: For Our People
lines: Be of good courage, and let us be courageous for our people, and for the cities of our God. — 2 Samuel 10:12
Courage is for somebody. Know who yours is for.

title: The Lion Prowls
lines: Be sober-minded; be watchful. Your adversary the devil prowls around like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour. — 1 Peter 5:8
Stay alert on the trail.

title: Hands That Don't Go Weak
lines: But you, take courage! Do not let your hands be weak, for your work shall be rewarded. — 2 Chronicles 15:7
Keep swinging. The wage is coming.

title: Fight the Good Fight
lines: Fight the good fight of the faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called. — 1 Timothy 6:12
Some fights are worth every round.

title: Established and Guarded
lines: But the Lord is faithful. He will establish you and guard you against the evil one. — 2 Thessalonians 3:3
The anchor holds whether you feel it or not.

title: Trained for Battle
lines: Blessed be the LORD, my rock, who trains my hands for war, and my fingers for battle. — Psalm 144:1
Strength is trained, not wished for.

title: I Will Uphold You
lines: Fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my righteous right hand. — Isaiah 41:10
The strongest grip on the rope isn't yours.
